sglang.title: [Fix] Handle nixlRemoteDisconnectError in NixlKVSender
sglang.author: cctry
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
The problem
nixlRemoteDisconnectError is raised by the NIXL library when the remote RDMA peer disconnects. It maps from UCX errors: UCS_ERR_NOT_CONNECTED, UCS_ERR_CONNECTION_RESET, UCS_ERR_ENDPOINT_TIMEOUT, UCS_ERR_CANCELED. Without this fix, any decode-side crash or network drop during KV transfer

sglang.author: merrymercy
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Summary

Add --random-input-len and --random-input-vocab-size flags to send_one.py
When --random-input-len is set, generates random token IDs as input instead of text prompts, avoiding radix cache hits — useful for profiling to ensure full prefill is captured
Each request in a batch gets unique rand

sglang.title: [NIXL][XPU] Use np.uint64 for pointer/length arrays in disaggregation KV transfer
sglang.author: Jianhong-Zhang
sglang.bodyExcerpt: Intel XPU device addr. pointers can exceed np.int64 range (bit 63 set), causing overflows and incorrect arithmetic when using numpy. This patch ensures all pointer and length arrays in the NIXL disaggregation backend are cast to np.uint64 up front, preventing overflow and dtype promotion issues duri

sglang.title: [HiSparse] Support FP8 KV cache by routing to flashmla_kv backend
sglang.author: whybeyoung
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
flashmla_sparse does not accept FP8 input, so HiSparse was previously pinned to BF16 KV. The flashmla_kv kernel already supports native FP8 + sparse attention (is_fp8_kvcache=True + indices=...), and HiSparse's hot-buffer indices are drop-in compatible with its indices contract.
Related resource:
#1

Motivation
In /health_generate endpoint,  healthcheck requests do not wait for the final output but return as soon as any response is received from the scheduler/detokenizer.
As noted in the code:
# As long as we receive any response from the detokenizer/scheduler, we consider the server is healthy.

This will also marginally improve eager performance as some of these asserts were in the forward pass.

Purpose
This PR upgrade mistral-common to 1.11.2. This allows to:

use 'reasoning' inside assistant message to support user sending back think trace to the model which improves performance for some of them (including Mistral Medium 3.5)
move Tool special handling inside mistral-common to reduce LOC

vllm.title: [Build] Fall back to system libgomp when torch has no vendored copy
vllm.author: lyd1992
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Summary
vllm_prepare_torch_gomp_shim() only populates VLLM_TORCH_GOMP_SHIM_DIR when the installed PyTorch ships a vendored libgomp (the pip wheels under site-packages/torch.libs/). When PyTorch is built from source or installed from a distro package — common on RISC-V, s390x, and Fedora / RHEL aarch

vllm.title: [Fix] Add missing stubs from cpu fp8 attention changes
vllm.author: tianmu-li
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
#39445 is missing some stubs for fp8 attention, which cause compilation errors when using clang (see https://github.com/tianmu-li/vllm/actions/runs/25149522905/job/73716695058#logs). This PR adds them.

vllm.title: [Spec Decode] Allow multimodal models with a warning
vllm.author: laviier
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Allow server to start up when speculative decoding with parallel drafting is enabled for multimodal models like Gemma-4
Previously, _raise_if_multimodal() unconditionally blocked parallel drafting for any model registered as multimodal, even when only text inference was needed. This change d

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Skip PP sampled-token receive on last rank during async scheduling
vllm.author: wi-adam
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Summary

avoid calling the PP sampled-token receive path on the last pipeline-parallel rank during async scheduling
keep the receive path on non-last PP ranks, which still need sampled token IDs broadcast by the last rank
preserve the existing lazy PP-group lookup when async scheduling is disabled
